summary: Marked the Heart Container item object as matching by flipping melee/it/items/itheart.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and correcting seven GALE01 symbol-table entries to local @ labels. The PR contained no C source edits; the matching enablement depended on build configuration plus symbol locality/naming fixes. decomp.dev reported GALE01 linked code 30.67% (+0.04%, +1744 bytes) and linked data 28.80% (+0.01%, +144 bytes).
key_files: configure.py: +1/-1; changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itheart.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itheart.c") in MatchingFor.
config/GALE01/symbols.txt: +7/-7; converted seven data/sdata/sdata2 entries from it_80... labels to local @ labels and, where needed, scope:local.
what_changed: Marked melee/it/items/itheart.c as Matching in configure.py.
Changed .data:0x803F5718 from it_803F5718 scope:global string to @234 scope:local string.
Changed .sdata:0x804D5210 and .sdata:0x804D5218 from it_804D5210/it_804D5218 scope:global strings to @188/@189 scope:local strings.
Changed .sdata2:0x804DC8A8 and .sdata2:0x804DC8AC from it_804DC8A8/it_804DC8AC labels to @156/@157 while preserving scope:local float metadata.
Changed .sdata2:0x804DD970 and .sdata2:0x804DD974 from it_804DD970/it_804DD974 scope:global float labels to @162/@163 scope:local floats.
Preserved the affected symbols' addresses, sections, sizes, alignments, and data type annotations; only names/scopes changed.
No source file diff was present for itheart.c in this PR.
smart_moves: Kept the PR tightly scoped to the minimum metadata needed to link the already-decompiled object: configure.py plus symbols.txt.
Used compiler-style local @NNN symbol labels for unidentified local constants instead of leaving them as address-derived global it_80... names.
Separated symbol visibility/naming corrections from source-code edits, making the matching-status change easy to audit.
Retained exact symbol addresses and size/type metadata, reducing risk while fixing only the locality information required for matching.
decomp_lessons: Before flipping an object to Matching, audit config/GALE01/symbols.txt for local data constants that were auto-named as global it_80... labels.
Local strings/floats in .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 should be represented as @NNN with scope:local when that is what the object expects.
A file can be match-ready without C source changes; the remaining blocker may be symbol-map scope/name metadata.
For small item-object matching PRs, decomp.dev linked code/data deltas are useful confirmation that the configure.py status flip actually linked bytes.
naming_conventions: Address-derived it_80... names in symbols.txt should represent global symbols, not anonymous/local constants.
Unidentified local data labels use @NNN form, as shown by @234, @188, @189, @156, @157, @162, and @163.
Item object paths in configure.py use lowercase filenames under melee/it/items/, e.g. melee/it/items/itheart.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly rewrite, C rewrite, or inline tactic is evidenced in the diff.
The matching tactic was symbol-table correction plus changing the build object classification from NonMatching to Matching.
The affected symbol metadata covered local strings and floats across .data, .sdata, and .sdata2.
decomp.dev measured the result as +1744 linked code bytes and +144 linked data bytes for GALE01.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review-thread feedback were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] posted a GALE01 report: linked code 30.67% (+0.04%, +1744 bytes), linked data 28.80% (+0.01%, +144 bytes), followed by 'No changes'.
follow_up_queries: Find item PRs that flip Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) with only configure.py and symbols.txt changes.
Search for symbols.txt changes converting it_80 address labels to @NNN scope:local labels.
Review future it/items matching blockers for local .sdata2 float labels using global names.
Compare itheart.c matching-status update patterns with itstar.c, itbat.c, and other common item objects.
